templates.name: Shelly 2PM Gen4
templates.description:
Monitoring template for Shelly 2PM Gen4 and compatible Shelly Gen2+ 2-channel power metering devices.

HYBRID MODE SUPPORT:
- Relay/switch mode: uses switch:0 and switch:1 component data.
- Cover/roller/door mode: uses cover:0 component data.

Uses a single HTTP agent master item polling /rpc/Shelly.GetStatus every {$SHELLY.INTERVAL}. Per-channel, cover, and system metrics are dependent items using JSONPath or JavaScript preprocessing.

AUTHENTICATION:
Uses DIGEST authentication for protected Shelly Gen2+ RPC endpoints. Configure host macros:
  {$SHELLY_HOST} - Shelly IP/DNS without protocol, e.g. 192.168.1.50
  {$SHELLY_USER} - username, usually admin
  {$SHELLY_PASS} - Shelly device password

NOTES:
If the device is configured as cover/roller/door, Shelly.GetStatus returns cover:0 instead of switch:0/switch:1. Switch-mode item preprocessing discards missing switch values cleanly, and cover-mode items collect the relevant metrics.

TUNABLE MACROS:
  {$SHELLY.INTERVAL}        - Poll interval (default: 60s)
  {$SHELLY.TEMP.MAX}        - Max temperature °C (default: 75)
  {$SHELLY.RSSI.MIN}        - Min WiFi RSSI dBm (default: -80)
  {$SHELLY.POWER.MAX.CH0}   - Max power threshold channel 0 W (default: 2300)
  {$SHELLY.POWER.MAX.CH1}   - Max power threshold channel 1 W (default: 2300)
  {$SHELLY.POWER.MAX.COVER} - Max power threshold cover/door motor W (default: 2300)
  {$SHELLY.NODATA.TIME}     - Time before unreachable trigger fires (default: 5m)

items.name: Shelly: Get full status (master)
items.description: Master item: polls /rpc/Shelly.GetStatus via HTTP GET. Protected Shelly Gen2/Gen3/Gen4 RPC calls normally require HTTP Digest authentication when device authentication is enabled. Returns full JSON with switch:0, switch:1, sys, wifi, cloud, input:0, input:1. All dependent items parse this single response.

items.name: Shelly: Get device info (master)
items.description: Master item: polls /rpc/Shelly.GetDeviceInfo every 6 hours. This RPC method may be available without authentication even when auth is enabled, so a successful response does not prove that credentials are valid for Shelly.GetStatus. Returns model, firmware version, MAC, auth status.

items.name: System: Uptime
items.description: Seconds since last device reboot.

items.name: System: RAM free
items.description: Available free RAM in bytes.

items.name: System: RAM total
items.description: Total RAM size in bytes.

items.name: System: Filesystem free
items.description: Free filesystem space in bytes.

items.name: System: Restart required
items.description: Set to 1 when the device requires a reboot to apply a configuration change.

items.name: System: Stable firmware update available
items.description: 1 if a stable firmware update is available for this device, 0 otherwise.

parameters: try { var u = JSON.parse(value); return (u.stable !== undefined) ? 1 : 0; } catch(e) { return 0; }

items.name: WiFi: Signal strength (RSSI)
items.description: WiFi received signal strength indicator. Typical good: >= -70 dBm. Poor: <= -80 dBm.

items.name: WiFi: Connected SSID
items.description: SSID of the WiFi network the device is currently connected to.

items.name: WiFi: IP address
items.description: Current IPv4 address assigned to the device on the WiFi network.

items.name: Channel 0: Relay state
items.description: Current output state of relay channel 0: 1=ON, 0=OFF.

items.name: Channel 0: Active power
items.description: Active (real) power consumption on channel 0 in Watts.

items.name: Channel 0: Voltage
items.description: AC mains voltage measured at channel 0 in Volts.

items.name: Channel 0: Current
items.description: Load current through channel 0 relay in Amperes.

items.name: Channel 0: AC frequency
items.description: Mains AC frequency measured at channel 0 in Hertz.

items.name: Channel 0: Power factor
items.description: Power factor at channel 0 (dimensionless, range 0.0-1.0). Value is discarded if not present in API response (some firmware versions omit this field).

items.name: Channel 0: Total energy consumed
items.description: Cumulative total energy consumed by channel 0 load in Watt-hours. Resets only on device factory reset.

items.name: Channel 0: Relay temperature
items.description: Internal temperature of the relay/switching element on channel 0 in Celsius.

items.name: Channel 0: Last control source
items.description: The last source that changed channel 0 relay state. Possible values: HTTP_in, timer, init, WS_in, MQTT, input, loopback, UI, RPC.

items.name: Channel 1: Relay state
items.description: Current output state of relay channel 1: 1=ON, 0=OFF.

items.name: Channel 1: Active power
items.description: Active (real) power consumption on channel 1 in Watts.

items.name: Channel 1: Voltage
items.description: AC mains voltage measured at channel 1 in Volts.

items.name: Channel 1: Current
items.description: Load current through channel 1 relay in Amperes.

items.name: Channel 1: AC frequency
items.description: Mains AC frequency measured at channel 1 in Hertz.

items.name: Channel 1: Power factor
items.description: Power factor at channel 1 (dimensionless, range 0.0-1.0). Value is discarded if not present in API response.

items.name: Channel 1: Total energy consumed
items.description: Cumulative total energy consumed by channel 1 load in Watt-hours. Resets only on device factory reset.

items.name: Channel 1: Relay temperature
items.description: Internal temperature of the relay/switching element on channel 1 in Celsius.

items.name: Channel 1: Last control source
items.description: The last source that changed channel 1 relay state. Possible values: HTTP_in, timer, init, WS_in, MQTT, input, loopback, UI, RPC.

items.name: Input 0: Physical switch state
items.description: State of physical input terminal 0 (button/switch). 1=pressed/closed, 0=released/open. Discarded if the field is absent.

items.name: Input 1: Physical switch state
items.description: State of physical input terminal 1 (button/switch). 1=pressed/closed, 0=released/open. Discarded if the field is absent.

items.name: Device: Model
items.description: Hardware model identifier (e.g. SNSW-102P16EU for Plus 2PM).

items.name: Device: Firmware version
items.description: Short firmware version string (e.g. 1.4.2).

items.name: Device: Firmware build ID
items.description: Full firmware build identifier including build date and git hash.

items.name: Device: Authentication enabled
items.description: 1 if HTTP authentication is currently enabled on the device, 0 if the device is unprotected.

items.name: Device: Active component mode
items.description: Detects whether Shelly.GetStatus exposes cover:0, switch:0/switch:1, or neither. Useful for distinguishing cover/roller/door mode from relay mode.

parameters: var o = JSON.parse(value); if (o['cover:0'] !== undefined) return 'cover'; if (o['switch:0'] !== undefined || o['switch:1'] !== undefined) return 'switch'; return 'unknown';

items.name: Cover 0: State
items.description: Cover/door state: open, closed, opening, closing, stopped, or calibrating.

items.name: Cover 0: Active power
items.description: Active power consumption of the cover/door motor in Watts.

items.name: Cover 0: Voltage
items.description: AC mains voltage measured for the cover/door output.

items.name: Cover 0: Current
items.description: Load current used by the cover/door motor in Amperes.

items.name: Cover 0: AC frequency
items.description: Mains AC frequency measured for the cover/door output.

items.name: Cover 0: Power factor
items.description: Power factor for the cover/door motor.

items.name: Cover 0: Total energy consumed
items.description: Cumulative total active energy consumed by the cover/door output in Watt-hours.

items.name: Cover 0: Temperature
items.description: Internal temperature reported for the cover/door component in Celsius.

items.name: Cover 0: Last control source
items.description: The last source that changed the cover/door state.

items.name: Cover 0: Last direction
items.description: Last movement direction reported by the cover component.

items.name: Cover 0: Current position
items.description: Current calibrated cover position, 0=fully closed and 100=fully open. Discarded when the device is uncalibrated or position is unknown. If Shelly does not currently expose current_pos, the item returns -1 and the value map displays Unknown / not available.

items.name: Cover 0: Target position
items.description: Target cover position, 0=fully closed and 100=fully open. Discarded when not available. If no active target_pos is exposed, the item returns -1 and the value map displays Unknown / not available.

items.name: Cover 0: Position control available
items.description: 1 if valid calibration data is available and position control can be used, 0 otherwise.

parameters: var c = JSON.parse(value)['cover:0']; if (!c || c.pos_control === undefined) throw 'cover pos_control not available'; return c.pos_control ? 1 : 0;

items.name: Cover 0: Error list
items.description: Comma-separated cover error list, or none when no cover errors are reported.

parameters: var c = JSON.parse(value)['cover:0']; if (!c) throw 'cover not present'; return (Array.isArray(c.errors) && c.errors.length) ? c.errors.join(',') : 'none';

items.name: Cover 0: Error count
items.description: Number of active cover error conditions.

parameters: var c = JSON.parse(value)['cover:0']; if (!c) throw 'cover not present'; return Array.isArray(c.errors) ? c.errors.length : 0;

macros.description: Shelly IP address or DNS name without protocol, e.g. 192.168.1.50.
HTTP username for Shelly device authentication. Default is usually admin.
HTTP password for Shelly device authentication. Required for Shelly.GetStatus when auth is enabled.
Polling interval for the /rpc/Shelly.GetStatus master item. All dependent items inherit this cadence.
Maximum acceptable relay temperature in Celsius before a WARNING trigger fires.
Minimum acceptable WiFi RSSI in dBm before a WARNING trigger fires.
Maximum power threshold for channel 0 in Watts. 2PM Gen4 is rated for 10A / ~2300W per channel.
Maximum power threshold for channel 1 in Watts.
Maximum power threshold for cover/door motor in Watts.
Duration without a successful poll before the device-unreachable trigger fires. Should be several multiples of {$SHELLY.INTERVAL}.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Device is unreachable
triggers.description: The device has not returned data for {$SHELLY.NODATA.TIME}. Check power, network connectivity, and that the device IP is reachable.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Channel 0 relay temperature too high (>={$SHELLY.TEMP.MAX}°C)
triggers.description: Channel 0 internal relay temperature has reached or exceeded {$SHELLY.TEMP.MAX}°C. The device may throttle or cut power to protect itself.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Channel 1 relay temperature too high (>={$SHELLY.TEMP.MAX}°C)
triggers.description: Channel 1 internal relay temperature has reached or exceeded {$SHELLY.TEMP.MAX}°C. The device may throttle or cut power to protect itself.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: WiFi signal is poor (RSSI < {$SHELLY.RSSI.MIN} dBm)
triggers.description: WiFi signal strength has dropped below {$SHELLY.RSSI.MIN} dBm. This may cause intermittent connectivity or polling failures.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Device restart required
triggers.description: The device has flagged that a reboot is required to apply a pending configuration change.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Device has rebooted
triggers.description: The device uptime counter has reset, indicating an unexpected or scheduled reboot. Uptime is currently under 10 minutes.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Channel 0 power consumption exceeds threshold (>{$SHELLY.POWER.MAX.CH0} W)
triggers.description: Active power on channel 0 has exceeded the configured threshold of {$SHELLY.POWER.MAX.CH0} W. Check the connected load.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Channel 1 power consumption exceeds threshold (>{$SHELLY.POWER.MAX.CH1} W)
triggers.description: Active power on channel 1 has exceeded the configured threshold of {$SHELLY.POWER.MAX.CH1} W. Check the connected load.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Stable firmware update is available
triggers.description: The device API reports a new stable firmware version is available. Update via the Shelly app or web UI at http://{$SHELLY_HOST}

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Cover temperature too high (>={$SHELLY.TEMP.MAX}°C)
triggers.description: The cover/door component temperature has reached or exceeded {$SHELLY.TEMP.MAX}°C.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Cover power consumption exceeds threshold (>{$SHELLY.POWER.MAX.COVER} W)
triggers.description: Active power on the cover/door motor has exceeded the configured threshold of {$SHELLY.POWER.MAX.COVER} W.

triggers.name: Shelly 2PM: Cover reports active error condition
triggers.description: The cover/door component reports one or more active error conditions. Check the Cover 0: Error list item.
